pages.title: Observability for shipping teams
pages.description: Dense, fast, AI-aware observability workflows built for developers.

sections.title: Observability that keeps up with you.
sections.body: Logs, traces, metrics, and LLM generations in one dense surface. Built around real engineer workflows, not generic dashboards.

items.title: Logs that scale
items.body: Virtualised explorer with live tail, server-side facets, and structured filtering.

items.title: Fast traces
items.body: Flamegraphs, service maps, and span detail without waiting on re-renders.

items.title: Metric queries
items.body: Aggregations, formulas, and time-series tuned with uPlot for 60 fps panning.

items.title: AI workflows
items.body: First-class LLM generations, sessions, prompts, and evals — not bolted on.

items.title: Actionable alerts
items.body: Rule builder, silences, and routing that stay readable at 1,000 rules.

items.title: OTLP native
items.body: Send from anything OpenTelemetry speaks — no agents, no lock-in.

sections.title: Ready when you are.
sections.body: Sign in to explore. No credit card for the evaluation tier.

pages.title: A tighter surface for logs, traces, metrics, and AI
pages.description: Keep the Datadog-grade density, remove the sprawl, make the happy path obvious.

sections.title: Dense. Fast. Navigable.
sections.body: Every page is shaped by real workflows — not screenshots of competitor dashboards. Shareable URLs, stable facets, and zero modal mazes.

items.title: Structured log search
items.body: Server-side facets, regex, and boolean operators. Click a value, never leave the page.

items.title: Flamegraph + span table
items.body: Correlate a slow span with its logs in one click. Critical-path spans are highlighted.

items.title: Formula-driven charts
items.body: Aggregate, ratio, threshold — all inline. No nested editors.

items.title: Generation explorer
items.body: Dataset runs, eval scores, and prompt revisions side-by-side.

items.title: Silences that make sense
items.body: Scope by service, instance, or arbitrary label. Expire automatically.

items.title: URL-shareable state
items.body: Paste a link, land on the exact view — filters, time range, drawer selection.

sections.title: Built to disappear
sections.body: Frontend that gets out of the way. Keyboard-first, dense by default, one shortcut palette for everything.
highlights: Command palette covers every route and entity.
All explorers share facets, analytics, and visualisations.
Time range, team, and theme persist across sessions.

pages.title: Predictable pricing for engineering teams
pages.description: Usage-aware pricing for infrastructure telemetry and LLM workloads.

sections.title: Pay for what you ingest. Nothing else.
sections.body: No per-seat games. No mystery add-ons. One price for telemetry volume, one for LLM generations.

items.title: Telemetry GB
items.body: Logs, traces, and metrics billed by compressed ingest. Same rate across types.

items.title: LLM generations
items.body: Per-generation billing for AI workloads, with dataset evaluations included.

items.title: Unlimited users
items.body: Invite the whole team. Roles + audit logs in every tier.

items.a: Yes — for evaluation workloads, with the same feature set as paid tiers.
No. Every tier includes unlimited users and roles.

items.q: What counts as an LLM generation?
items.a: A single completion call. Streaming partials are rolled up into one generation.
Yes — see the Self-host page for details on the license.

pages.title: Built around compatibility, density, and speed
pages.description: A frontend shaped around real observability workflows instead of generic dashboards.

sections.title: Density and speed aren't afterthoughts.
sections.body: Every layer — query defaults, virtualisation, chart primitives, WebSocket buffering — is budgeted for Datadog-scale workloads from the first commit.

items.title: uPlot everywhere
items.body: No heavy chart runtime. 60 fps panning at 10k points per series.

items.title: Virtualised explorers
items.body: Live-tail and paginated views render only visible rows.

items.title: Query-standard defaults
items.body: One wrapper for staleTime, retry, and keepPreviousData — no flashing skeletons.

items.title: Feature isolation
items.body: ESLint-enforced. No cross-feature imports. Shared code lives in @shared.

sections.title: Budgeted from day one
sections.body: The frontend holds to an explicit architecture budget enforced in CI.
highlights: Chunk size budgets on every feature.
No TS enums. No as any. No cross-feature imports.
Strict loading-state convention: isPending && data === undefined.
Zustand for UI state, TanStack Query for server state — never mixed.

pages.title: OpenTelemetry in, fast insight out
pages.description: Adopt OTLP without losing the navigability and workflow depth engineers actually need.

sections.title: OTLP is the only contract.
sections.body: Point any OpenTelemetry exporter at the collector and the platform picks up traces, logs, and metrics. No agents. No vendor SDKs.

sections.title: Send your first span
sections.body:
export OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT=https://otlp.example.com
export OTEL_SERVICE_NAME=checkout

# That's it. Any OTel SDK will start flowing to Optikk.

items.title: Full OTLP spec
items.body: Spans, logs, metrics — HTTP and gRPC, batched and streaming.

items.title: Semantic conventions
items.body: Service maps and instrumentation inference rely on the OTel spec directly.

items.title: No SDK lock-in
items.body: Switch vendors tomorrow — your instrumentation is standards-based.

pages.title: Self-host when control matters
pages.description: Run the platform close to your telemetry while keeping the same dense UI model.

sections.title: Bring the platform to your telemetry.
sections.body: Run Optikk inside your VPC with the same UX as the managed tier. Same binaries, same charts, same query engine.

items.title: Single binary
items.body: One Go binary per region. Horizontal scale on commodity hardware.

items.title: Your keys, your rules
items.body: Bring your own KMS, OIDC, and audit sink. Zero data leaves your network.

items.title: Same UI
items.body: No feature splits between cloud and self-host — the frontend is identical.

sections.title: Get the self-host pack.
sections.body: Helm chart, Terraform module, and a runbook.
